name: Roberta Flack
abstract: Roberta Cleopatra Flack (February 10, 1937 – February 24, 2025) was an American singer and pianist known for her emotive, genre-blending ballads that spanned R&B, jazz, folk, and pop and contributed to the birth of the quiet storm radio format. Her commercial success included the Billboard Hot 100 chart-topping singles "The First Time Ev…

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: So we actually signed her on Les’ say- so... So I signed her... And you know, if you want to know like how I do it? Like this... Listen I had a hit with Roberta Flack and Donny Hathaway that Arif Martin and I co- produced.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: Hall & Oates, Simon & Garfunkel, Roberta Flack, Donnie Hathaway, everybody. When I really look at the overall picture I’ve had more fun than a barrel of monkeys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: We had Basie, Billy Eckstine, Sammy Davis, Joe Williams, Roberta Flack, Sarah Vaughan, Aretha Franklin, Peggy Lee, Jimmy Cleveland.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Peggy Lee, Sarah Vaughan, Aretha Franklin -- Roberta Flack. [INTERVIEWEE] That's right. That's who it was.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: >> [INTERVIEWEE] That’s Roberta Flack, right? ... He did a couple other people. Then he eventually did Roberta Flack.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: The first one I had was Duke Ellington, Roberta Flack -- she was hot then, she had made this big hit -- Cal Tjader.
